title: digitalis mariana subsp. heywoodii
intro: Digitalis mariana subsp. heywoodii, also known as Foxglove, is a biennial plant belonging to the Plantaginaceae family, native to the western Mediterranean region. It contains glycosides, specifically digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used for centuries for their medicinal properties, including treating heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The plant's health benefits include improving cardiovascular function, reducing symptoms of heart failure, and potentially lowering blood pressure. Digitalis mariana subsp. heywoodii can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or powder, often used in herbal medicine to treat various heart-related conditions.
